channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-23
item.description:
Our open-source research community has seen significant progress across multiple projects. Notably, the `scienceverse/metacheck` project has resolved a critical code_check crash and enhanced its paper database loading capabilities, thanks to Lakens' contributions. Meanwhile, the `giladfeldman/docpluck` project has improved its documentation and table rendering, particularly for the RC-T Layer-2 feature. Additionally, the `shaheedazaad/auto-zcurve` and `giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward` projects have made important updates, including a revised validation note with a citation and the addition of a Chrome Webstore link, respectively. These individual advancements demonstrate the community's commitment to improving research tools and highlight the connections between projects, such as the shared goal of enhancing research databases and documentation. Overall, these updates showcase the community's collaborative spirit and dedication to advancing open-source research.

102 commits across 6 active repositories this week.

Dormant repos revived: output-it-forward

Most active:

regcheck32 commits
coarse30 commits
docpluck26 commits
metacheck10 commits
escicheck3 commits

item.title: docpluck: 3 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

The `feat/rc-t-table-region-guard` branch was worked on, where documentation was added for "RC-T Layer-2 + combined-ship closeout (cycle 2, v2.4.97)" in the learnings section. The same branch also saw fixes to tables, specifically addressing "DP-2 blank p/df + DP-5 two-header-row recovery/super-header alignment + RC-T Layer-2 raw_text prose-trim/suppress" in version "v2.4.97". Additionally, a rendering fix was made in the `feat/rc-t-table-region-guard` branch to "strip Camelot tables that are absorbed body prose" in version "v2.4.96" for "RC-T".

3 commit(s) today:

  • 3e9cc2c [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(learnings): RC-T Layer-2 + combined-ship closeout (cycle 2, v2.4.97) — Gilad Feldman
  • 813aa4c [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] fix(tables): v2.4.97 — DP-2 blank p/df + DP-5 two-header-row recovery/super-header alignment + RC-T Layer-2 raw_text prose-trim/suppress — Gilad Feldman
  • 84a4d42 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] fix(render): RC-T — strip Camelot tables that are absorbed body prose (v2.4.96) — Gilad Feldman
  • 7247285 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(handoff+todo): RC-T implementation handoff + log RC-T deferral / close collabra.77859 adjudication — Gilad Feldman
  • 927d869 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(spec): RC-T table-region prose-contamination — confirmed 2-layer root cause + fix plan — Gilad Feldman
  • db7192b [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(triage+learnings): v2.4.95 corpus assessment — 7/7 canary FAIL, RC-B7 already done (W0h), RC-T root-caused — Gilad Feldman
  • 2dbdd98 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs+tooling: enforce library↔app version-pin sync (directive + qa/review/deploy gate) — Gilad Feldman
  • c1fa68b [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(todo): log v2.4.95 / REQUEST_11 shipped + deferred follow-ups (effect_type opt-in, 77859 caption-number, text-layer caveat) — Gilad Feldman
  • 370b89c [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(handoff): record the cross-session skill-file commit coordination (now fulfilled in b2c6aa2) — Gilad Feldman
  • b2c6aa2 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] chore(docs+deploy): prior-session housekeeping — REQUEST_10 customer broadcast, daily-digest cron-retry lesson, deploy smoke-gate fix — Gilad Feldman
  • bce4b7f [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] feat(tables): REQUEST_11 — flatten fields for non-clinical result tables (v2.4.95) — Gilad Feldman
  • 83ab6bf [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(deploy): document pre-push canary hook timing — never timeout/kill the push — Gilad Feldman
  • 1158b1b [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] feat(tables): REQUEST_10 — HTTP-flatten enabling quality fixes + cross-flavor capture (v2.4.94) — Gilad Feldman
  • 2fd8711 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(iterate): 2026-06-18 run handoff (v2.4.92 shipped, caption-guard reverted, paused at architectural boundary) — Gilad Feldman
  • c671929 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(iterate): cycle 2 (v2.4.92 affiliation guard) + cycle 3 (caption-follows REVERTED) journal — Gilad Feldman
  • cf420ca [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] feat(render): never promote affiliation lines to subsection headings (v2.4.92) — Gilad Feldman
  • f392957 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(iterate): v2.4.91 shipped handoff + cycle journal — Gilad Feldman
  • 7edc62f [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] chore(canary): onboard efendic/maier/xiao into rotating pool (rotation now active) — Gilad Feldman
  • 662c4df [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] @ — Gilad Feldman
  • cfe86f7 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] feat: add optional extraction guardrails for size and timeout — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 2 commits today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

The staging branch was the focus of recent work, with a commit fixing OSF and mutlistudy bugs, as stated in the message "fix OSF + mutlistudy bugs". Another commit on the staging branch aimed to "harden security features", enhancing the repository's security. No other branches were worked on during this period, with all changes confined to the staging branch. The fixes and security hardening were both done by Jamie Cummins. The specific bugs fixed in OSF and mutlistudy are not detailed, but they were addressed in the first commit. The security features that were hardened are also not specified, but the intention was to increase security.

2 commit(s) today:

  • 5b53ad6 [staging] fix OSF + mutlistudy bugs — Jamie Cummins
  • 71be3be [staging] harden security features — Jamie Cummins
  • fbd5030 [staging] firm up qwen formatting — Jamie Cummins
  • f775856 [staging] improve qwen — Jamie Cummins
  • 724d020 [staging] re-add qwen! — Jamie Cummins
  • 89a1470 [staging] push for redeploy — Jamie Cummins
  • baebc81 [staging] hunting down osf bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 2f5eaaa [staging] improve logging — Jamie Cummins
  • 7fb80fe [staging] improve error message — Jamie Cummins
  • a2f7302 [staging] fix osf bug — Jamie Cummins
  • d2089d5 [staging] remove groq endpoints — Jamie Cummins
  • 51b9ba6 [staging] harden json handling — Jamie Cummins
  • 6cdde08 [staging] fix oss bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 1fd2de7 [staging] use more efficient storage — Jamie Cummins
  • 4b7a527 [staging] patch oss SDK handler — Jamie Cummins
  • e514303 [staging] fix oss handling bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 599489b [staging] harden qwen handling — Jamie Cummins
  • 8e7e452 [staging] improve embedding efficiency, purge dead code — Jamie Cummins
  • 88e039c [staging] replace deprecated llama — Jamie Cummins
  • 80dcf34 [staging] harden osf handling again — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →

item.title: output-it-forward: activity update
item.description:
giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward

The `main` branch was updated with a commit that added a Chrome Webstore link for OIF, as stated in the commit message "Add Chrome Webstore link for OIF". No other branches were worked on in this set of commits. The specific change made was the addition of a link, with no other features or fixes mentioned. There were no changes to existing modules or addition of new ones, aside from the added link. The commit message does not mention any other branches or areas of work being affected. The work was limited to a single commit on the `main` branch.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
